title: Holopogon pantlingii
taxonomy: Holopogon pantlingii is classified within the family Aspleniaceae, a group of ferns commonly known as the maidenhair ferns. This family is part of the larger division Pteridophyta, which encompasses all ferns and their allies. The Aspleniaceae family is characterized by its unique frond structure and leaflet arrangement. intro: Holopogon pantlingii, commonly known as Himalayan or Indian pennywort, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the family Urticaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, improving cognitive function, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Holopogon pantlingii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
